technique: PowerShell Process with Reflective Code Injection
evidence: volatility_pstree.json: powershell.exe PID 1744 (PPID 4880 explorer.exe) created 2026-06-10T18:36:20Z, spawned NETSTAT.EXE and conhost.exe
volatility_malfind.json: PID 468 has 3 RWX memory regions with shellcode stubs (jmp rax pattern)
volatility_malfind.json: PID 1744 has 3 RWX memory regions with shellcode stubs (jmp rax pattern)
description: Two PowerShell processes (PIDs 468, 1744) launched from explorer.exe contain injected shellcode (RWX memory regions with jmp rax API resolution stubs). PID 1744 executed netstat for network reconnaissance. These processes were running during the attack window and are the likely parent/launcher for the LSASS dump operation. The absence of a process creation event for rundll32.exe within the attack window suggests the dump was performed inline by one of these PowerShell processes using .NET P/Invoke or direct DLL invocation.

evidence: mft_timeline.json: C:/ProgramData modified at 2026-06-14T19:19:19Z (mac) - consistent with dump file write location
sysmon.json: Event 7 (Image Load) at 2026-06-14T19:19:17.483Z - likely comsvcs.dll loaded into dumping process
sysmon.json: Event 10 (Process Access) x2 at 2026-06-14T19:19:19.823Z - process opening handle to lsass.exe
description: Dump file named lsass-comsvcs.dmp was recovered from disk. The filename directly indicates the comsvcs.dll MiniDump technique. MFT shows C:\ProgramData was modified (mac) at 19:19:19Z, within the attack window and matching the expected dump file write. Sysmon Event 7 shows an image load at 19:19:17.483Z immediately before the dump, likely comsvcs.dll loading into the dumping process. Sysmon Event 10 shows process access events targeting lsass.exe at 19:19:19.823Z and 19:19:20.153Z, consistent with the dump operation. Only one Event 1 (process creation) exists in the entire window, suggesting the dump was performed by an already-running process (likely PID 1744 powershell.exe, created June 10) using direct API calls or PowerShell reflection rather than spawning rundll32.exe. No rundll32.exe was found in the memory snapshot process tree.
